features/APIs"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-08-23T04:13:26Z","updated_at":"2024-10-10T00:07:17Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description

Currently, `NpzFile` returned from `cupy.load` does not implement `Mapping` protocol (e.g., `get()`, `items()`, etc. are missing). It would be better to support these methods.

CuPy: https://github.com/cupy/cupy/blob/v13.3.0/cupy/_io/npz.py#L11-L29
NumPy: https://numpy.org/devdocs/reference/generated/numpy.lib.npyio.NpzFile.html

Also it looks NumPy 2.0 made the class documented. We should follow that as well.

### Additional Information

I noticed this when porting [llama3.np](https://github.com/likejazz/llama3.np) for CuPy. If this is implemented, `llama3.np` will run on CuPy just by rewriting `import numpy as np` to `import cupy as np`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8557/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8557/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/comments/2403646955","html_url":"https://github.com/cupy/cupy/issues/8557#issuecomment-2403646955","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/8557","id":2403646955,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBFKgkM6PRL3r","user":{"login":"arashandishgar","id":42874930,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyODc0OTMw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42874930?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar","html_url":"https://github.com/arashandishgar","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-10T00:07:15Z","updated_at":"2024-10-10T00:07:15Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Hi,

Currently, numpy `NpzFile` inherits from `collections.abc.Mapping, `but cupy `NpzFile` inherits from object. If cupy `NpzFile` were to inherit from collections.abc.Mapping and implement the required methods which include `__iter__` and `__len__`, this problem would be completely resolved.

Thus, I believe there are two potential solutions for this issue. One option is to implement the methods you've mentioned (`get()`,`items()`, etc.). The second option is to have `NpzFile` inherit from `collections.abc.Mapping.`

Which solution do you think would be better for this issue?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/cupy/cupy/issues/comments/2403646955/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

+import collections.abc
 import warnings
 
 import numpy
 
 import cupy
 
-
 _support_allow_pickle = (numpy.lib.NumpyVersion(numpy.__version__) >= '1.10.0')
 
 
-class NpzFile(object):
+if numpy.lib.NumpyVersion(numpy.__version__) >= '2.0.0':
+    from numpy.lib._npyio_impl import BagObj","path":"cupy/_io/npz.py","commit_id":"a9093658798a2410fcdf16954b0bad798ddd015a","original_commit_id":"a9093658798a2410fcdf16954b0bad798ddd015a","user":{"login":"arashandishgar","id":42874930,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyODc0OTMw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42874930?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar","html_url":"https://github.com/arashandishgar","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/arashandishgar/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":""Initially, I came up with the idea of having a separate BagObj class.
